title: Discrete Mathematics - Set Theory and Algebra - Relations

questions.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

The union of two equivalence relations is also an equivalence relation.


GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2d

How many binary relations are there on a set \( A \) with \( n \) elements?


GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 9a

How many true inclusion relations are there of the form \( A \subseteq B \), where \( A \) and \( B \) are subsets of a set \( S \) with \( n \) elements?


GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 9e

The transitive closure of the relation \( \left\{(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4), (5, 4)\right\} \) on the set \( \left\{1, 2, 3, 4, 5\right\} \) is ___________.


GATE CSE 1989 | Question: 1-iv

Let \( S \) be the set of all integers and let \( n > 1 \) be a fixed integer. Define for \( a,b \in  S, a R b \) iff \( a-b \) is a multiple of \( n \). Show that \( R \) is an equivalence relation and find its equivalence classes for \( n = 5 \).


GATE CSE 1992 | Question: 15.b

Amongst the properties \( \left\{\text{reflexivity, symmetry, anti-symmetry, transitivity}\right\} \) the relation \( R=\{(x, y) \in N^2|x \neq y\} \) satisfies _________


GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 2.3

Let \( R \) be a symmetric and transitive relation on a set \( A \). Then



GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 1.19
options: \( R \) is reflexive and hence an equivalence relation
\( R \) is reflexive and hence a partial order
\( R \) is reflexive and hence not an equivalence relation

questions.question:
Let \( R \) be a non-empty relation on a collection of sets defined by \( _{A}R_ B \) if and only if \( A \cap B = \phi \). Then, (pick the true statement)



GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 2.2
options: \( R \) is symmetric and not transitive
\( R \) is not reflexive and not symmetric
questions.correct_answer: \( R \) is symmetric and not transitive

questions.question:
Let \( F \) be the collection of all functions \( f: \{1, 2, 3\} \to \{1, 2, 3\} \). If \( f \) and \( g \in F \), define an equivalence relation \( \sim \) by \( f\sim g \) if and only if \( f(3) = g(3) \). 

  1. Find the number of equivalence classes defined by \( \sim \). 
  2. Find the number of elements in each equivalence class. 


GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 8

Let \( R \) be a reflexive and transitive relation on a set \( A \). Define a new relation \( E \) on \( A \) as

\( E=\{(a, b) \mid (a, b) \in R \text{ and } (b, a) \in R \} \)

Prove that \( E \) is an equivalence relation on \( A \).

Define a relation \( \leq \) on the equivalence classes of \( E \) as \( E_1 \leq E_2 \) if \( \exists a, b \) such that \( a \in E_1, b \in E_2 \text{ and } (a, b) \in R \). Prove that \( \leq \) is a partial order.


GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 14

The number of equivalence relations of the set \( \{1,2,3,4\} \) is



GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 6.3

Suppose \( A \) is a finite set with \( n \) elements. The number of elements in the largest equivalence relation of A is



GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 1.6

Let \( R_1 \) and \( R_2 \) be two equivalence relations on a set. Consider the following assertions:

  1. \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) is an equivalence relation
  2. \( R_1 \cap R_2 \) is an equivalence relation

Which of the following is correct?



GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 1.7
options: Assertions (i) is true but assertions (ii) is not true
Assertions (ii) is true but assertions (i) is not true
questions.correct_answer: Assertions (ii) is true but assertions (i) is not true

questions.question:
Prove by induction that the expression for the number of diagonals in a polygon of \( n \) sides is \( \frac{n(n-3)}{2} \)


GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 10a

Let \( R \) be a binary relation on \( A = \{a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h\} \) represented by the following two component digraph. Find the smallest integers \( m \) and \( n \) such that \( m < n \) and \( R^m = R^n \).



GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 10b

The number of binary relations on a set with \( n \) elements is:



GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 1.2

Let \( L \) be a set with a relation \( R \) which is transitive, anti-symmetric and reflexive and for any two elements \( a, b \in  L \), let the least upper bound \( lub (a, b) \) and the greatest lower bound \( glb (a, b) \) exist. Which of the following is/are true?



GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 2.3

1. Mr. X claims the following: 

    If a relation R is both symmetric and transitive, then R is reflexive. For this, Mr. X offers the following proof:

    “From xRy, using symmetry we get yRx. Now because R is transitive xRy and yRx together imply xRx. Therefore, R is reflexive”.

  2. Give an example of a relation R which is symmetric and transitive but not reflexive.


GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 3

A relation \( R \) is defined on the set of integers as \( xRy \) iff \( (x + y) \) is even. Which of the following statements is true?



GATE CSE 2000 | Question: 2.5
options: \( R \) is not an equivalence relation
\( R \) is an equivalence relation having \( 1 \) equivalence class
\( R \) is an equivalence relation having \( 2 \) equivalence classes
\( R \) is an equivalence relation having \( 3 \) equivalence classes
questions.correct_answer: \( R \) is an equivalence relation having \( 2 \) equivalence classes

questions.question:
Consider the following relations:

  • \( R_1\:(a,b) \) iff \( (a+b) \) is even over the set of integers
  • \( R_2 \:(a,b) \) iff \( (a+b) \) is odd over the set of integers
  • \( R_3 \:(a,b) \) iff \( a.b > 0 \) over the set of non-zero rational numbers
  • \( R_4\:(a,b) \) iff \( |a-b| \leq 2 \) over the set of natural numbers

Which of the following statements is correct?



GATE CSE 2001 | Question: 1.2
options: \( R_1 \) and \( R_2 \) are equivalence relations, \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are not
\( R_1 \) and \( R_3 \) are equivalence relations, \( R_2 \) and \( R_4 \) are not
\( R_1 \) and \( R_4 \) are equivalence relations, \( R_2 \) and \( R_3 \) are not
\( R_1, R_2, R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) all are equivalence relations
questions.correct_answer: \( R_1 \) and \( R_3 \) are equivalence relations, \( R_2 \) and \( R_4 \) are not

questions.question:
The binary relation \( S= \phi \text{(empty set)} \) on a set \( A = \left \{ 1,2,3 \right \} \) is 



GATE CSE 2002 | Question: 2.17

Let \( A \) be a set of \( n(>0) \) elements. Let \( N_r \) be the number of binary relations on \( A \) and let \( N_f \) be the number of functions from \( A \) to \( A \)



GATE CSE 2002 | Question: 3
options: Give the expression for \( N_r, \) in terms of \( n. \)
Give the expression for \( N_f, \) terms of \( n. \)
Which is larger for all possible \( n,N_r \) or \( N_f \)

questions.question:
Consider the binary relation:

\( S= \left\{\left(x, y\right) \mid y=x+1 \text{ and } x, y \in \left\{0, 1, 2\right\} \right\} \)

The reflexive transitive closure is \( S \) is



GATE CSE 2004 | Question: 24

Let \( R \) and \( S \) be any two equivalence relations on a non-empty set \( A \). Which one of the following statements is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2005 | Question: 42
options: \( R \) \( ∪ \) \( S \), \( R \) \( ∩ \) \( S \) are both equivalence relations
\( R \) \( ∪ \) \( S \) is an equivalence relation
\( R \) \( ∩ \) \( S \) is an equivalence relation
Neither \( R \) \( ∪ \) \( S \) nor \( R \) \( ∩ \) \( S \) are equivalence relations
questions.correct_answer: \( R \) \( ∩ \) \( S \) is an equivalence relation

questions.question:
The time complexity of computing the transitive closure of a binary relation on a set of \( n \) elements is known to be:



GATE CSE 2005 | Question: 7

A relation \( R \) is defined on ordered pairs of integers as follows: \[ (x,y)R(u,v) \text{ if } x<u \text{ and } y>v \] Then \( R \) is:



GATE CSE 2006 | Question: 4
options: Neither a Partial Order nor an Equivalence Relation
A Partial Order but not a Total Order
questions.correct_answer: Neither a Partial Order nor an Equivalence Relation

questions.question:
Let \( S \) be a set of \( n \) elements. The number of ordered pairs in the largest and the smallest equivalence relations on \( S \) are:



GATE CSE 2007 | Question: 2

Consider the binary relation \( R = \left\{(x,y), (x,z), (z,x), (z,y)\right\} \) on the set \( \{x,y,z\} \). Which one of the following is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2009 | Question: 4
options: \( R \) is symmetric but NOT antisymmetric
\( R \) is NOT symmetric but antisymmetric
\( R \) is both symmetric and antisymmetric
\( R \) is neither symmetric nor antisymmetric
questions.correct_answer: \( R \) is neither symmetric nor antisymmetric

questions.question:
What is the possible number of reflexive relations on a set of \( 5 \) elements?



GATE CSE 2010 | Question: 3

Let \( R \) be the relation on the set of positive integers such that \( aRb \) and only if \( a \) and \( b \) are distinct and let have a common divisor other than \( 1. \) Which one of the following statements about \( R \) is true?



GATE CSE 2015 Set 2 | Question: 16
options: \( R \) is symmetric and reflexive but not transitive
\( R \) is reflexive but not symmetric not transitive
\( R \) is transitive but not reflexive and not symmetric
\( R \) is symmetric but not reflexive and not transitive
questions.correct_answer: \( R \) is symmetric but not reflexive and not transitive

questions.question:
Let \( R \) be a relation on the set of ordered pairs of positive integers such that \( ((p,q),(r,s)) \in R \) if and only if \( p-s=q-r \). Which one of the following is true about \( R \)?



GATE CSE 2015 Set 3 | Question: 41

A binary relation \( R \) on \( \mathbb{N} \times \mathbb{N} \) is defined as follows: \( (a, b) R(c, d) \) if \( a \leq c \) or \( b \leq d \). Consider the following propositions:

  • \( P: \) \( R \) is reflexive.
  • \( Q: \) \( R \) is transitive.

Which one of the following statements is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2016 Set 2 | Question: 26
options: \( P \) is true and \( Q \) is false.
\( P \) is false and \( Q \) is true.
questions.correct_answer: \( P \) is true and \( Q \) is false.

questions.question:
Let \( \mathcal{R} \) be the set of all binary relations on the set \( \{1,2,3\} \). Suppose a relation is chosen from \( \mathcal{R} \) at random. The probability that the chosen relation is reflexive (round off to \( 3 \) decimal places) is ______.


GATE CSE 2020 | Question: 17

A relation \( R \) is said to be circular if \( a\text{R}b \) and \( b\text{R}c \) together imply \( c\text{R}a \).

Which of the following options is/are correct?



GATE CSE 2021 Set 1 | Question: 43
options: If a relation \( S \) is reflexive and symmetric, then \( S \) is an equivalence relation.
If a relation \( S \) is circular and symmetric, then \( S \) is an equivalence relation.
If a relation \( S \) is reflexive and circular, then \( S \) is an equivalence relation.
If a relation \( S \) is transitive and circular, then \( S \) is an equivalence relation.
questions.correct_answer: If a relation \( S \) is reflexive and circular, then \( S \) is an equivalence relation.

questions.question:
​​Let \( \mathcal{F} \) be the set of all functions from \( \{1, \ldots, n\} \) to \( \{0,1\} \). Define the binary relation \( \leqslant \) on \( \mathcal{F} \) as follows:

\( \forall f, g \in \mathcal{F}, f \preccurlyeq g \) if and only if \( \forall x \in\{1, \ldots, n\}, f(x) \leq g(x) \), where \( 0 \leq 1 \).
Which of the following statement(s) is/are TRUE?



GATE CSE 2025 | Set 2 | Question: 32

Let \( R_{1} \) be a relation from \( A = \left \{ 1,3,5,7 \right \} \) to \( B = \left \{ 2,4,6,8 \right \} \) and \( R_{2} \) be another relation from \( B \) to \( C = \{1, 2, 3, 4\} \) as defined below:

An element \( x \) in \( A \) is related to an element \( y \) in \( B \) (under \( R_{1} \)) if \( x + y \) is divisible by \( 3. \)

An element \( x \) in \( B \) is related to an element \( y \) in \( C \) (under \( R_{2} \)) if \( x + y \) is even but not divisible by \( 3. \)

Which is the composite relation \( R_{1}R_{2} \) from \( A \) to \( C \)?

\( R_{1}R_{2}  =  \{(1, 2), (1, 4), (3, 3), (5, 4), (7, 3)\} \)

\( R_{1}R_{2} =  \{(1, 2), (1, 3), (3, 2), (5, 2), (7, 3)\} \)

\( R_{1}R_{2} =  \{(1, 2), (3, 2), (3, 4), (5, 4), (7, 2)\} \)

\( R_{1}R_{2}  =  \{(3, 2), (3, 4), (5, 1), (5, 3), (7, 1)\} \)


GATE IT 2004 | Question: 4
